description_long: This object is a solar energy infrastructure installation composed of eight rectangular blue photovoltaic panels arranged in a 2x4 grid. The panels are framed with metallic borders and are mounted on a robust central support structure, which appears to be a pole with a cylindrical base. Electrical cabling is visible, running from the underside of the panels down the support pole, suggesting a connection to an inverter or power distribution unit. The surface of the panels is smooth and glossy, with a grid of fine lines indicating individual solar cells. The mounting structure is likely made of metal, providing stability and durability for outdoor urban environments.
description: A large 2x4 grid of blue solar panels mounted on a central pole with visible cabling and a sturdy metallic frame.
description_view_0: Angled view showing the blue solar panels' grid pattern and the central support pole beneath.
description_view_1: Direct underside view highlighting the black cabling, central mounting structure, and the panel arrangement from below.
description_view_2: Angled view similar to view 0 but from the opposite side, showing the cabling and support pole.
description_view_3: Top-down view displaying the full 2x4 grid of blue solar panels and their metallic frames.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 30 degrees, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: pole-mounted with a central steel or aluminum support structure
attribute_cabling_visibility: black and red cables visible running from the underside of the panels down the central pole
attribute_array_configuration: 2x4 grid of solar panels arranged in two rows and four columns
